description: Bonyad-e Taavon-e Sepah (IRGC Cooperative Foundation) is the primary economic and business cooperative arm of the [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps). It controls vast economic interests across multiple sectors of the Iranian economy including construction, manufacturing, and trade. The foundation serves as a vehicle for the IRGC's deep penetration of Iran's civilian economy. [Parviz Fattah](/people/parviz-fattah) served as executive director of its investment arm after leaving the Energy Ministry and before becoming deputy commander of [Khatam al-Anbiya](/orgs/khatam-al-anbiya). The US Treasury sanctioned Bonyad Taavon Sepah and Fattah in December 2010 for its role in the IRGC's economic empire.

sources.title: Treasury Designates Iranian Entities Tied to the IRGC and IRISL

editorial_notes: Also transliterated as Bonyad Taavon Sepah. US-sanctioned December 2010. Parviz Fattah was its executive director (investment arm). Distinct from Khatam al-Anbiya (construction) but both are IRGC economic arms.
